message:
fix(learn): sanitize ReactMarkdown props to prevent invalid DOM attributes

ReactMarkdown passes internal AST props (node, siblingCount, index, ordered,
isHeader, inline) to custom component functions. When these props are spread
to DOM elements, they create invalid HTML attributes like:

  <h2 node="[object Object]" siblingcount="3">

This breaks Tailwind's prose CSS attribute selectors, causing headings, lists,
and other elements to render without proper styling - making the lesson pages
look broken.

This commit introduces a dedicated markdown-components library that:

1. Strips all ReactMarkdown internal props before passing to DOM elements
2. Demotes heading levels (h1→h2, h2→h3, etc.) since pages have their own h1
3. Adds external link handling (target="_blank" for http:// links)
4. Provides premium code block styling with:
   - Language badge detection from className (e.g., "language-bash")
   - Copy-to-clipboard button with visual feedback
   - Proper overflow handling
5. Includes complete table component support with proper styling
6. Replaces inline eslint-disable comments with proper TypeScript typing

The lesson-content.tsx component is updated to:
- Import the new markdownComponents library
- Remove rehype-highlight (styling now handled by custom Pre component)
- Update prose classes for proper heading hierarchy (h2-h4 instead of h1-h3)
- Add prose-strong styling for bold text visibility

Files:
- apps/web/lib/markdown-components.tsx (new)
- apps/web/app/learn/[slug]/lesson-content.tsx (modified)
